examples.title: Calculate Memory Capacity
examples.description: Calculate maximum memory capacity based on address and data bus width
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateMemoryCapacity
INPUT: addressBits, dataBits
BEGIN
  addressSpace = 2 ^ addressBits
  wordSize = dataBits / 8
  capacity = addressSpace * wordSize
  
  IF capacity >= 1073741824 THEN
    PRINT capacity/1073741824, "GB"
  ELSE IF capacity >= 1048576 THEN
    PRINT capacity/1048576, "MB"
  ELSE IF capacity >= 1024 THEN
    PRINT capacity/1024, "KB"
  ELSE
    PRINT capacity, "bytes"
  ENDIF
END
examples.explanation: Given address bus bits (n) and data bus bits (m), calculates maximum memory that can be addressed. Address space = 2^n locations, each holding m/8 bytes.

examples.title: Demonstrate Byte Ordering
examples.description: Show how integers are stored in Big-Endian and Little-Endian formats
examples.code:
import struct

def demonstrate_endian():
    number = 0x12345678
    
    # Convert to bytes
    big_endian = number.to_bytes(4, byteorder='big')
    little_endian = number.to_bytes(4, byteorder='little')
    
    print(f"Number: {hex(number)}")
    print(f"Big-Endian bytes: {' '.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in big_endian)}")
    print(f"Little-Endian bytes: {' '.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in little_endian)}")
    
    # Show address layout
    print("\nAddress 0  1  2  3")
    print(f"Big:      {big_endian[0]:02x}  {big_endian[1]:02x}  {big_endian[2]:02x}  {big_endian[3]:02x}")
    print(f"Little:   {little_endian[0]:02x}  {little_endian[1]:02x}  {little_endian[2]:02x}  {little_endian[3]:02x}")

demonstrate_endian()
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how multi-byte integers are stored in different byte orders. Big-Endian stores most significant byte at lowest address; Little-Endian stores least significant byte first.

examples.title: Memory Address Calculation
examples.description: Calculate byte address and word address for given memory configuration
examples.explanation: Calculates memory organization parameters including total capacity, word address from byte address, and byte offset within a word. Demonstrates address translation concepts.

examples.title: Simulate Direct Cache Mapping
examples.description: Demonstrate how main memory blocks map to cache lines in direct mapping
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DirectCacheMapping
INPUT: mainMemoryBlockNumber, cacheSize, blockSize
BEGIN
  numberOfLines = cacheSize / blockSize
  lineIndex = mainMemoryBlockNumber MOD numberOfLines
  tag = mainMemoryBlockNumber DIV numberOfLines
  
  PRINT "Main Memory Block:", mainMemoryBlockNumber
  PRINT "Maps to Cache Line:", lineIndex
  PRINT "Tag Value:", tag
  PRINT "---"
END

ALGORITHM DemonstrateMapping
BEGIN
  cacheLines = 8    // 8 cache lines
  blockSize = 4     // 4 words per block
  
  FOR block = 0 TO 15 DO
    lineIndex = block MOD cacheLines
    tag = block / cacheLines
    PRINT "Block", block, "-> Line", lineIndex, "(Tag:", tag, ")"
  ENDFOR
END
examples.explanation: Direct mapping uses modulo operation to map memory blocks to cache lines. Each block has only one possible cache line location. This creates potential conflicts when blocks with same line index are accessed frequently.
